title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels
description: A report labels len(text) as characters, counts newline and combining marks as letters, merges case categories without policy, and presents incomparable metrics under one total. Learners cumulatively produce honestly labeled text-metrics panel through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
